title: rubus australis monstr. squarrosus
intro: Rubus australis monstr. squarrosus, also known as the New Zealand thimbleberry, is a rare and unique species of bramble that belongs to the rose family. This plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus australis monstr. squarrosus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and provide relief from pain and discomfort. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
